title: A nonnegative row-stochastic row is infinity-norm stable

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: A nonnegative two-entry row whose weights sum to one maps inputs bounded by M in absolute value to an output bounded by M.
informal.latex: a,b >= 0, a+b=1, |x|,|y| <= M implies |a x + b y| <= M.
assumptions: a and b are nonnegative real row weights
informal.expected_conclusion: The row output is bounded in absolute value by M.

declarations.name: row2Apply
declarations.statement: def row2Apply (a b x y : Real) : Real := a * x + b * y

declarations.name: row2Apply_abs_le
declarations.statement: theorem row2Apply_abs_le (a b x y M : Real) (ha : 0 <= a) (hb : 0 <= b) (hsum : a + b = 1) (hx : |x| <= M) (hy : |y| <= M) : |row2Apply a b x y| <= M

tasks.input: Translate infinity-norm stability of a nonnegative row-stochastic row into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem row2Apply_abs_le (a b x y M : Real) (ha : 0 <= a) (hb : 0 <= b) (hsum : a + b = 1) (hx : |x| <= M) (hy : |y| <= M) : |row2Apply a b x y| <= M

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof using abs_add_le, abs_mul, nonnegativity of weights, and the row-sum identity.
tasks.target: Unfold row2Apply, bound the absolute value by weighted absolute values, use hx/hy, then rewrite a+b=1.
